machine_components.name: DMG MORI DMC 80 FD duoBLOCK (5-axis mill-turn)
Rotary table / C axis (FD DirectDrive turning table)
Flood coolant system (KSS)
machine_components.$energy_note: Pump is electrical/low-energy by itself, but coolant system carried energy_bearing=true per 05-safety-loto: coolant/high-pressure pumps are LOTO-controlled (unexpected pump start, high-pressure spray).

machine_components.name: SINUMERIK Safety Integrated (SI) / E-Stop circuit & door interlocks
Operator panel / HMI (ERGOline X / CELOS, SINUMERIK Operate)
Work-offset & tool-data screens (Parameter area: work offsets, tool list, magazine list)
Active tool library / TO unit (the loaded recipe for the job)

failure_modes.title: Limit / working-area alarm at cycle start (machine won't move)

observations: Alarm fires the instant cycle start is pressed; machine has not moved
Alarm text names a travel/axis (operators recall Z named)
Often first job of the morning, after a power-down, or a new setup / Monday
operator_phrases: limit alarm, machine hasn't even moved

operational_context.operation: OP20 / first part of new setup or first job after power-down

causes.mechanism: One or more axes not referenced/homed after power-down — control does not know position, refuses motion (cf. 10203 NC start without reference point). Reference symbols missing on the position page.
causes.$seed_prior_basis: PROVISIONAL — not SME-elicited. Joe & veteran both cite unreferenced axis as the usual cause; Joe self-checks reference symbols each morning; ~monthly per operator, more shop-wide after power-downs/Mondays. High emphasis.

causes.mechanism: Active work offset (G54 part zero) is wrong/zeroed or was bumped — first commanded position lands beyond a software or working-area limit. Distinct from tool offset (length). NOT to be silenced by widening limits.
causes.$seed_prior_basis: PROVISIONAL — not SME-elicited. Peter: G54 part zero wrong on new setup (re-probed, ~20 min with Denver); veteran: 'someone bumped the offset.' Every-few-weeks/operator, ~weekly shop-wide. Slightly below unreferenced-axis because that is operators' most-cited first cause.

failure_modes.anti_pattern: Do NOT widen the software limit or 'nudge'/eyeball an offset to make the move reach, and do not reflex reset-and-rerun (Peter and veteran both did this; Peter was stopped — re-running a wrong zero re-presents it at the part). A 10620 software-limit hit is machine protection; do not relax without engineering/AS9100 change control.

failure_modes.title: 5-axis orientation fault — head won't go to that angle (wrong library for fixture rev)

observations: Program runs in, then stops dead at a motion block (operators cite N490 on the OP20 bracket)
Alarm/text: orientation not possible / can't do that angle; head won't tilt
Bracket / forging-variant family; rising since the new fixture revision came in
Library names look near-identical, differ by revision suffix (e.g. LIB-083 vs LIB-087)
operator_phrases: the head won't go to that angle
picked the bracket program and it faulted at N490
wrong library for the fixture rev
program faults at a motion block

operational_context.selected_library: bracket-finish library (LIB-083 vs LIB-087 by fixture rev)

causes.mechanism: Wrong tool library / recipe loaded for the current fixture revision — the recipe was proven against the prior fixture rev (relocated locator/clamp). Part number unchanged so nothing alarms on identity; geometry/orientation no longer fits, head cannot reach the commanded angle. Identity mismatch, not a machine fault.
causes.$seed_prior_basis: PROVISIONAL — not SME-elicited. Co-anchored by Peter, Alex AND the veteran (veteran's #1: 'Wrong library. Every time.'); Denver's diagnosis on Peter's case; few times/month, rising since new fixture rev. Highest emphasis across interviews — top provisional prior.

causes.mechanism: Library name matches traveler but the kinematic transformation (TRAORI) the program assumed is not active/set up, or a swivel-plane/CYCLE800 orientation the running state doesn't have — a genuine program-side problem. Operator escalates; never toggles TRAORI or edits transformation data.
causes.$seed_prior_basis: PROVISIONAL — not SME-elicited. Research (03-5axis) names TRAORI not-active / CYCLE800 as the secondary class once identity matches; interviews route this to Denver ('if names line up, that's Denver'). Lower prior — operators rarely reach it because wrong-library dominates.

failure_modes.anti_pattern: Do NOT grab the closest-looking library off the list to keep moving (multiple operators report colleagues doing this — 'how you buy a titanium paperweight'), do not block-search into the middle of a 5-axis/transformed move to skip the stop, and do not jog a rotary axis to line it up. Veteran self-swaps without single-block on a known job — capture, do not endorse for juniors.

failure_modes.title: Wrong / missing tool data — tool not in magazine, length blank, or wrong length crash

observations: Program calls a tool and stops: 'tool not in magazine' / not available (tool in wrong pocket)
Tool physically present but length/data blank or zero in the tool list
No alarm but wrong depth/position, or a crash into the fixture (wrong length after a regrind)
operator_phrases: T42 wasn't where it should be
the length wasn't in the table
tool was there but the data wasn't in the list
wrong length, tool went right into the fixture
didn't check the tool data after a regrind

operational_context.operation: tool-change block / first cut after a tool swap or regrind

causes.mechanism: Tool physically in the wrong magazine pocket vs the setup sheet (pulled for another job, returned wrong). Pure identity/location mismatch — operator can correct by matching magazine to setup sheet.
causes.$seed_prior_basis: PROVISIONAL — not SME-elicited. Joe's T42-in-wrong-pocket: he self-remedies this every few weeks. Operator-fixable, frequent — moderate-high prior.

causes.mechanism: Tool length/diameter blank, stale, or wrong in the tool table (not updated after a regrind, or transcription error). Feeds a bad number to every cut: cuts air, wrong depth, or crash into fixture. Operator must NOT invent a value — programmer/Denver measures and sets.
causes.$seed_prior_basis: PROVISIONAL — not SME-elicited. Alex (once, length blank), veteran (crash after regrind, 'always tool data'). Veteran ranks it as a serious/crash-class item but personally rare for juniors; emphatic 'you do NOT make up a tool length.' Mid prior.

failure_modes.anti_pattern: Never hand-type / make up a tool length or diameter to clear the stop ('the fastest crash in the building'); never transfer wear into geometry; never pick a similar tool or renumber a pocket to dodge the alarm; do not keep cutting 'to see.'

failure_modes.title: Wrong tool library / TO unit loaded — tool name not defined at the change block

observations: Control stops at the tool-change block with a 'name not defined' type stop
Active tool list / library on screen is not the one named on the traveler
Among ~140 near-identically-named libraries, the wrong one is a few characters away
operator_phrases: the program set doesn't match the traveler
library name vs traveler doesn't line up

operational_context.operation: first tool-change block of the program
operational_context.selected_library: active TO unit vs traveler-named library (case-sensitive, revision suffix)

causes.mechanism: Operator selected the wrong tool library / TO unit for the job (picked from a pick-list of ~140 near-identical names; case-sensitive identifiers, revision suffix look-alikes). The named tool cannot resolve. Identity check (name on screen vs name on traveler) catches it before the first chip.
causes.$seed_prior_basis: PROVISIONAL — not SME-elicited. Distinct from the orientation fault (this surfaces at the tool-change/name-resolve stage, 12550, not a motion-block orientation stop). Grounded in 03-5axis (12550 class) + 10-tooling-library identity check; veteran/Peter library discipline. Moderate-high prior.

failure_modes.anti_pattern: Do not pick a similar/closest tool or renumber a pocket to clear the name-not-defined stop, and do not create/edit tool entries to make the alarm go away. A name mismatch is a revision-control question for the setter/programmer/quality, not an operator edit.

failure_modes.title: Protection / collision-zone violation (won't run; looks like the limit alarm)

observations: Alarm at cycle start or on a move; text says protection zone or working area; won't run
Looks a lot like the limit alarm — distinguished only by the alarm number

causes.mechanism: Programmed/look-ahead path would enter an NCK or channel protection zone. Operator does not override the zone — reads the alarm number to separate it from a software-limit hit, then routes to programmer/maintenance. Program/setup is reviewed, retracted, corrected by the owner.
causes.$seed_prior_basis: PROVISIONAL — not SME-elicited. Peter only: 'twice ever,' last spring, 'we don't mess with it.' Rare; low prior. Carried as a separate mode because research flags its signature overlaps the limit alarm (different number is the discriminator).

failure_modes.anti_pattern: Do not override or disable the protection zone to keep running, and do not block-search past it. The zone is a collision guard; correction is the program/setup owner's, not the operator's.

failure_modes.title: Safety Integrated safe-stop (STOP A–E / E-stop / NCK-PLC cross-check stop) — escalate, never override

observations: Machine performs a safe-stop (drives ramped down / coast-stopped); alarm in the 27xxx Safety Integrated family or a 30xx emergency-stop
Often after a door/interlock or E-stop event; cannot reset without clearing the underlying fault
Operators treat it as a straight maintenance call ('you don't touch those')
operator_phrases: door thing, the machine did its safe-stop
it stopped hard and won't reset

causes.mechanism: A safety channel demanded a safe state — a STOP A–E was triggered (alarms 27020–27024), an NCK↔PLC SI cross-check deviation stopped an axis (27090/27091/27092), a monitoring-channel error fired (27001), or an emergency stop was asserted (3000/3001, e.g. door/interlock/E-stop button). Points at the safety system itself, not a routine clear-and-rerun. Investigated by qualified maintenance, never overridden or force-reset.
causes.$seed_prior_basis: PROVISIONAL — not SME-elicited. Joe: 'once ever, straight maintenance call'; Peter: passing mention. Rare; lowest-but-present prior. guardrail=true forces escalate-always regardless of frequency. Codes grounded verbatim in 840Dsl_alarms_diagnostics_2019 (27020 'stop E triggered' … 27024 'stop A triggered'; 27090/27091/27092 NCK-PLC data cross-check; 27001 monitoring channel; 3000/3001 emergency stop).

failure_modes.anti_pattern: Never override, suppress, or force-reset a Safety Integrated / 27xxx safe stop or a 30xx E-stop to keep running; never treat E-stop/RESET/mode-switch as a lockout; never bypass or 'trick' a door interlock or light curtain. A safe stop is investigated, not cleared.

failure_modes.title: Safety Integrated: axis not safely referenced (safe cams/SI homing not established) — escalate, never override

observations: 27xxx alarm states an axis (or 'at least one axis') is NOT safely referenced — distinct from the ordinary NC reference symbol
Safe position-dependent functions (safe end position / safe cams) will not run until SI re-referencing is established
Operators must not 'reference past it' with an ordinary homing trick — SI referencing is a maintenance/commissioning function
operator_phrases: says safely referenced, not the normal reference thing

operational_context.operation: after power-up / SI startup, or after an encoder/hardware event

causes.mechanism: Safety Integrated does not have a valid safe reference for an axis (27000 'Axis is not safely referenced' / 27100 'At least one axis is not safely referenced'). Safe position-monitoring (safe end position, safe cams) cannot be guaranteed. SI re-referencing / user agreement is a qualified maintenance/commissioning task — NOT the ordinary JOG-REF the operator does each morning, and NOT something the operator force-clears.
causes.$seed_prior_basis: PROVISIONAL — not SME-elicited. No operator named this specifically; carried as a distinct SI mode because the manuals (840Dsl_alarms_diagnostics_2019: 27000 'Axis %1 is not safely referenced', 27100 'At least one axis is not safely referenced') describe a safety-referencing failure that LOOKS like an ordinary unreferenced-axis limit alarm but is escalate-only. Discriminator is the alarm number. Lowest prior; guardrail=true.

failure_modes.anti_pattern: Do NOT treat a 27000/27100 'not safely referenced' as the ordinary morning JOG-REF homing — it is a Safety Integrated reference, re-established only by qualified maintenance/commissioning. Never force-clear it or suppress the SI alarm to keep running.

failure_modes.title: Safety Integrated: safe-motion limit exceeded (SOS / safe standstill / safe velocity / safe end position) — escalate, never override

observations: 27xxx alarm says a safe limit was exceeded: safe standstill tolerance, safe velocity, safe end position, or a safe-operational-stop (SOS) NCK/drive difference
The drive has tripped a safe reaction; the move that exceeded the safe envelope is the thing to investigate
Operators record the number and call maintenance — never widen a safe limit
operator_phrases: 27-something, it moved when it shouldn't have

operational_context.operation: setup/handling with a safe function active, or a safe-stop condition during motion

causes.mechanism: A Safety Integrated motion monitor detected the axis outside its safe envelope: safe standstill tolerance exceeded (27010), safe velocity exceeded (27011), safe end position exceeded (27012), safe acceleration monitoring exceeded (27013), or an NCK↔drive difference in safe-operational-stop / safe-velocity (27101/27102). The cause is a genuine deviation from the parameterized safe behaviour (mechanics, parameterization, or a real over-travel/over-speed) — diagnosed by qualified maintenance. The operator never widens a safe limit or suppresses the monitor.
causes.$seed_prior_basis: PROVISIONAL — not SME-elicited. No operator named these; carried because the manuals (840Dsl_alarms_diagnostics_2019: 27010 'tolerance for safe standstill exceeded', 27011 'safe velocity exceeded', 27012 'safe end position exceeded', 27013 'Safe monitoring for acceleration exceeded', 27101/27102 SOS/safe-velocity NCK-vs-drive difference) describe the SI motion-monitoring class distinctly from the safe-stop class. Lowest prior; guardrail=true.

failure_modes.anti_pattern: Never widen, raise, or disable a safe standstill / safe velocity / safe end-position limit to clear a 27010–27013 / 27101 / 27102 alarm. These are safety envelopes; a violation is investigated by maintenance, not relaxed.

failure_modes.title: Safety Integrated: SI checksum / hardware-change — confirmation & acceptance test required — escalate, never override

observations: 27xxx alarm text explicitly says 'checksum error' and/or 'confirmation and acceptance test required', or 'new hardware component'
Indicates the SI safety configuration changed/diverged — a formal acceptance test (and sign-off) is required before safe operation resumes
Strictly a qualified-personnel acceptance-test event; never acknowledged-away by an operator
operator_phrases: says new hardware, confirm and test

operational_context.operation: after a parameter/firmware/hardware change to the drive or SI configuration

causes.mechanism: The Safety Integrated configuration changed or its checksum no longer matches the accepted reference: 27003 'Checksum error found', 27032 'checksum error of safe monitoring. Confirmation and acceptance test required', 27035 'new hardware component, confirmation and functional test required', 27060 'Drive assignment checksum error. Confirmation and acceptance test required', 27093 'Checksum error NCK-SPL'. Resolution is a formal SI confirmation + acceptance test by qualified personnel (and AS9100 documented sign-off) — never an operator acknowledgement.
causes.$seed_prior_basis: PROVISIONAL — not SME-elicited. No operator named these; carried because the manuals (840Dsl_alarms_diagnostics_2019) describe an SI checksum / hardware-change / acceptance-test class distinct from the run-time safe-stop and motion-monitoring classes. Lowest prior; guardrail=true. These specifically require a documented acceptance test, which is AS9100-relevant.

failure_modes.anti_pattern: Never 'acknowledge away' or force-clear a 27003/27032/27035/27060/27093 SI checksum / hardware-change alarm. These require a formal Safety Integrated acceptance test and sign-off by qualified personnel; an operator clear is never the answer.

failure_modes.title: OEM builder-band (7xxxxx) tool-changer fault — defer to DMG MORI

observations: Tool change aborts mid-cycle; gripper/arm did not retract
A 'big-number' alarm in the 700,000 range — not the normal Siemens kind
Sometimes the OEM text mentions a door or a filter; operators record the number and call maintenance
alarm_codes: <7xxxxx — DMG MORI builder band; meaning machine-specific; defer to DMG MORI docs / on-control text>
operator_phrases: big-number alarm, not the normal Siemens kind
I write the number down and call maintenance

causes.mechanism: A DMG MORI machine-builder PLC user alarm (700000-799999 band) — its meaning is NOT in Siemens documentation. Number-to-cause mapping is DMG MORI's to define. Read back the on-control OEM text and the visible clear/cancel symbol (those are trustworthy), record the number, and defer; never guess a cause or clearing action.
causes.$seed_prior_basis: PROVISIONAL — not SME-elicited. Joe: a couple times a quarter, last late Apr/early May, and the single biggest TIME cost (waits on maintenance, up to most of a shift) even though not the most frequent. Mid prior reflecting moderate frequency + high downtime weight.

failure_modes.anti_pattern: Never invent a cause, parameter meaning, or clearing action for a 7xxxxx number; never clear an unknown changer alarm with the spindle right there. The number maps to DMG MORI's docs only.

failure_modes.title: Coolant off-mix / low concentration — finish degrades and tools die (no alarm)

observations: No alarm — machine is mechanically happy
Surface finish degrades through a long titanium run; tools wear/die fast (swapping too often)
Coolant smells off; rusty films starting on the worktable/fixture area
operator_phrases: finish is going off and tools die fast
the coolant smells a bit off

operational_context.operation: long titanium run (e.g. long bracket run)

causes.mechanism: Coolant concentration has drifted below the product PDS band (top-ups with water, evaporation, tramp oil). Weak lubrication/heat control on low-thermal-conductivity titanium → edge burn, work-hardening, short tool life, poor finish, corrosion films. Soft failure with no alarm.
causes.$seed_prior_basis: PROVISIONAL — not SME-elicited. Alex's #2, a few times/month on long runs, last 2026-06-09 (rust film). 'Sneaky' but machine keeps running. Mid prior — frequent but low-severity-per-event; the source seed row ir_2026_0611_alex_02.

failure_modes.anti_pattern: Do not 'just add more coolant' / top up blindly without measuring on the refractometer (Alex's unmeasured top-up — captured, not endorsed); do not chase one reading; do not run titanium cuts dry; do not add water to concentrate (use 'oil in last', pre-mixed).

failure_modes.title: Chatter / vibration - poor surface finish (no alarm)

observations: No alarm - machine is mechanically fine
Poor / rough surface finish, visible chatter marks
Audible chatter or vibration during the cut

causes.mechanism: Worn or chipped cutting edge raises cutting forces and excites vibration; finish degrades, chatter marks appear. No alarm.
causes.$seed_prior_basis: PROVISIONAL - demo mode; worn insert is the usual first suspect for chatter.

failure_modes.anti_pattern: Do not just slow the feed/speed to 'mask' chatter without checking the insert - a worn edge keeps degrading and can break.

failure_modes.title: 4th-axis hydraulic clamp pressure loss — part creeps mid-cut (no alarm)

observations: No alarm — machine is mechanically happy, but the part physically shifts in the fixture mid-cut
Surface finish walks out of tolerance on a 4th-axis job
Clamp pilot pressure measured below spec at the fixture
Confirmed not coolant and not chatter — the clamp is losing grip
operator_phrases: it's not coolant and not chatter
the part physically shifts in the fixture mid-cut

causes.mechanism: Worn seal in the hydraulic clamp cylinder lets clamp pilot pressure bleed/drop below spec, so clamping force fades during the cut and the part creeps/shifts in the fixture. No alarm — a soft hydraulic failure. Resolved by replacing the clamp-cylinder seal and re-verifying that pressure holds at spec.
causes.$seed_prior_basis: First case (conv 9cc87d7e). Senior diagnosed a worn clamp-cylinder seal dropping pilot pressure; seal swap restored spec. seed_prior 0 — not SME-elicited.

failure_modes.anti_pattern: Do NOT keep running the program with clamp/fixture pilot pressure below spec to finish the part — a slipping 4th-axis clamp risks the part being thrown or a tool/fixture crash. Stop the cycle, verify clamp pilot pressure, and if it is below spec hand off to maintenance for the clamp-cylinder seal. Do not crank up pressure or 'top it off' to mask a failing seal, and never bypass a clamp/fixture pressure interlock.

checks.ask: Read me the alarm NUMBER on the Diagnosis screen exactly (digits, including any leading band), not just the words.
On the position page, does every axis show its reference/homed symbol, or is the little symbol missing on one or more slides?
answers: one or more missing the symbol

discriminates.cause_unreferenced_axis: one or more missing the symbol

checks.ask: On the Work Offset screen, does the ACTIVE work offset (e.g. G54) part-zero match the value/datum on the traveler/setup sheet? (Work offset = where the part is, NOT tool length.)
answers: does not match / looks bumped or zeroed

discriminates.cause_wrong_work_offset: does not match / looks bumped or zeroed

checks.ask: Read the tool-library/recipe name on the screen and the library name on the traveler character-for-character (case-sensitive, watch the revision suffix). Do they match exactly?
Does the revision stamped/tagged on the physical fixture match the fixture revision on the traveler?
On the Magazine list, is the called tool sitting in the pocket the setup sheet assigns it (e.g. T42 in pocket 12)?
On the Tool list / Tool Offset screen, is a length/diameter value actually present for the called tool, or is it blank/zero?
Compare the tool's length/diameter on the Tool Offset screen against the preset/touch-off sheet for THIS tool (especially after a regrind). Do they agree?
answers: does not match the preset sheet

discriminates.cause_tool_length_missing_or_wrong: does not match the preset sheet

checks.ask: Read back the on-screen OEM alarm TEXT and the visible clear/cancel symbol exactly as shown (do not interpret the number).
Using the refractometer (zeroed on clean water), sample from the delivery nozzle and read Brix; multiply by the product PDS factor. Is the concentration within the PDS band?

safety_gate.require: nitrile gloves + eye protection; sample at nozzle not stagnant sump

checks.ask: Look/smell the coolant: clean smell, no thick tramp-oil layer/scum/foam? Any rust films on parts/ways/worktable?
answers: off smell / tramp oil / rust films

discriminates.cause_coolant_low_concentration: off smell / tramp oil / rust films

checks.ask: Pull the insert and look at the cutting edge - is it chipped/worn, or still sharp?
On the hydraulic gauge for the 4th-axis clamp, read the clamp pilot pressure. Is it at the fixture spec, or below?

safety.require: work zone clear; know head/table/tool position before any motion

closeout.verify: All axes show the reference symbol; program runs past cycle start without the limit/no-reference alarm.
closeout.on_verify_fail: If it still alarms with axes referenced, check the work offset (proc_verify_work_offset) or escalate to Denver/programmer.

safety.require: do NOT widen the software limit or eyeball/nudge the offset to make the move reach

closeout.verify: Active G54 part zero matches the setup sheet datum.
closeout.on_verify_fail: If G54 matches the sheet but it still alarms, it may be the program — escalate to programmer (proc_escalate_offset_programmer). Do not reset-and-rerun.

handoff.action: Escalate to programmer (Denver): G54 matches the sheet but the limit alarm persists — possible program-side offset/frame issue. Operator does not edit the program.

safety.require: do NOT grab the closest-looking library; identity (name) check only, no content/tool-data edits

closeout.verify: Loaded library name matches the traveler (incl. revision suffix); program proves out past the orientation block.
closeout.on_verify_fail: If names match and it still faults at the orientation block, escalate to programmer (proc_escalate_orientation_programmer).
restore: return to start of program / sanctioned restart point

handoff.action: Escalate to programmer (Denver): library name matches the traveler but the 5-axis orientation block still faults — possible TRAORI/CYCLE800 transform-not-active or program issue. Operator never toggles TRAORI or edits transformation data.

safety.require: spindle stopped; tool changer at rest before handling pockets

closeout.verify: Called tool sits in the setup-sheet pocket; program resolves the tool and runs.
closeout.on_verify_fail: If the tool is truly missing or its data is blank, do not improvise — escalate (proc_escalate_tool_data).

handoff.action: Escalate to programmer (Denver): tool length/diameter is blank, wrong, or unverifiable (e.g. after a regrind). Operator must NOT invent a value; Denver measures and sets the data.
Escalate to programmer (Denver): library name matches the traveler but the named tool still won't resolve (12550), or option-related wording appears. Operator does not create/edit tool entries.
Escalate to programmer/maintenance: a protection/collision-zone alarm (10700-10704) — operator never overrides the zone. Program/setup is reviewed, retracted, and corrected by the owner.

STOP and escalate to maintenance: a Safety Integrated event — a 27xxx alarm (safe-stop / STOP A–E, safe-referencing, safe-motion limit, or SI checksum/acceptance-test) or a 30xx E-stop. Never override, suppress, force-reset, widen a safe limit, or 'acknowledge away' an acceptance-test alarm. Record the number and the event; investigate, do not clear-and-rerun. SI checksum / hardware-change alarms require a documented acceptance test by qualified personnel (AS9100).
capture_fields: preceding event (e.g. door, power-up, hardware/parameter change)

handoff.action: DEFER to DMG MORI: a 7xxxxx builder-band alarm has no Siemens meaning. Read back the on-control OEM text + clear symbol, record the number, and route to maintenance/DMG MORI docs. Never guess a cause or clearing action; never clear an unknown changer alarm with the spindle present.
capture_fields: what the changer was doing (e.g. arm didn't retract)

safety.require: nitrile gloves + eye protection; do not add water to concentrate (oil-in-last); do not add biocide without authorization

closeout.verify: Re-measured concentration sits within the product PDS band after circulation; finish/films improving.
closeout.on_verify_fail: If concentration is in-band but finish/tool-life is still off, or smell/pH is sour, flag to Denver/lead — do not dose biocide.

closeout.verify: Fresh insert fitted; a test pass gives a clean finish with no chatter.

safety.require: LOTO + dump hydraulic accumulator before opening the clamp cylinder

closeout.verify: Clamp pilot pressure holds at spec through a test clamp/unclamp; clamp grips through a proveout cut.
closeout.on_verify_fail: If pressure still drops, escalate further up maintenance — possible cylinder bore/valve issue.

steps.action: Confirm the work zone is clear and you know where the tool, spindle and B/C head/table are before any motion.
steps.expected_result: Operator confirms a clear zone and known machine position.
Control is in REF mode, ready to home axes.

steps.action: Reference (home) each axis that is missing its symbol, traversing toward the reference point.
steps.expected_result: Each axis reaches its reference point; smooth even travel, no following-error alarm.

steps.action: Confirm every axis now shows its reference symbol on the position page.
Open the Work Offset (zero offset) screen and identify the ACTIVE offset (e.g. G54).
steps.expected_result: Active work offset and its part-zero values are displayed.

steps.action: Compare the active G54 part zero against the traveler/setup-sheet datum (work offset = where the part is, not tool length). Do not nudge or widen anything.
steps.expected_result: Either G54 matches the sheet (escalate if still alarming) or a clear mismatch is found.

steps.action: Read the program name, tool-library name, and fixture ID+revision verbatim from the traveler, then read the active program and loaded library name on the control. Compare character-for-character (case-sensitive, watch the revision suffix and look-alikes 0/O, 1/l).
steps.expected_result: A definite match or mismatch of names is established.

steps.action: If the names do not agree, load the library named on the traveler for this fixture revision. Do NOT pick the closest-looking one.
steps.expected_result: The library named on the traveler is the active TO unit.

steps.action: Prove out from a sanctioned restart point in single block, feed/rapid override down with a hand on feed-hold; do not block-search into the middle of a transformed move.
steps.expected_result: Program passes the previously faulting orientation block under controlled feed.

steps.action: On the Magazine list, find the called tool and compare its pocket against the setup-sheet assignment.
steps.expected_result: Tool's actual pocket vs assigned pocket is known.

steps.action: If the tool is in the wrong pocket, place it in the setup-sheet pocket (spindle stopped, changer at rest).
steps.expected_result: Called tool is in its assigned pocket; the program resolves it.

steps.action: Zero the handheld refractometer on clean water (ideally the same DI/RO water used to mix coolant); wipe the prism dry.
steps.expected_result: Refractometer reads zero on clean water (calibrated).

steps.action: Sample coolant from the delivery nozzle (not a stagnant sump corner), read Brix at the light/dark boundary, multiply by the product PDS factor to get concentration %.
steps.expected_result: A concentration % value, compared against the PDS band. A blurry boundary means unstable emulsion/tramp oil — clean up and re-sample.

steps.action: If too low, add pre-mixed coolant (richer than target) following oil-in-last; if too high, add DI/RO water gradually. Make small changes; circulate before re-measuring.
steps.expected_result: Concentration moves toward the PDS band; re-measure after circulation rather than chasing one reading.

steps.action: Record the concentration reading and top-up amount in the coolant log.
steps.expected_result: Coolant log updated (the trend matters more than any single reading).

steps.action: Swap to a fresh insert (same grade/geometry), seat it properly, then run a test pass at the same feed and speed.
warnings_before: Spindle stopped and safe before reaching in

warnings.text: Never jog blind or cycle-start without knowing tool, spindle and 5-axis (B/C) positions and that the work zone is clear — especially after a stop, block-search, or offset change.

Never widen a software/overtravel limit, and never eyeball/nudge an offset to make a move reach. A limit hit is a real position fault / machine-protection boundary, not a setting to relax (AS9100 change control).

Never load the closest-looking tool library/recipe to keep moving. A name mismatch (case-sensitive, revision suffix) is a revision-control question for the setter/programmer/quality — not an operator edit. On titanium this is how a high-value forging gets scrapped.

Do not block-search into the middle of a 5-axis/transformed move to skip a stop — the assumed transformation/frame and rotary angles may not be re-established and the tool can plunge into the part or fixture.
Never hand-type or make up a tool length/diameter to clear a stop — it is the fastest crash in the building. If the data is blank/wrong, escalate; the programmer measures and sets it.

Never override, suppress, or force-reset a Safety Integrated / 27xxx safe stop, and never bypass a door interlock or treat E-stop/RESET/mode-switch as a lockout. A safe stop is investigated, not cleared.
Never invent a cause or clearing action for a 7xxxxx DMG MORI builder-band alarm; read back the on-control OEM text + clear symbol and defer to DMG MORI docs/maintenance.
Wear nitrile gloves + eye protection with coolant; add concentrate to water (oil-in-last), never water to concentrate; never dose biocide without authorization; never run titanium cuts dry.

never_do: a length i think is right
